docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: This section explains how to create a new notebook in GoodNotes and customize its cover and internal template. This task is essential for users starting their digital note-taking journey.

docs.title: Create a New Notebook
docs.text:
1. Launch the app

When you open the GoodNotes 6 app, you will see the empty Documents page which will be served as your homepage. You can start creating a new notebook and folders in Documents section.

  1. Click the ‘+’ icon

Choose the “NOTEBOOK” options from the menu on the top. + 여러 options이 잇다. - annotate Notebook button

  1. Configure notebook settings
  2. name
  3. language

  4. cover toggle

  5. Choose Cover design

  6. choose from cover templates

    • you can also import your own templates
    • you can go to marketplace and purchase one you like

  7. each template has its unique set of colours you can choose from

  8. you can choose from different sizes (A4, Letter)

eg. Notebook name : COMM 2216

  1. Select Paper template
  2. similar to cover, you can select various templates (essesntials, writing paper, music sheets, planner)

  3. can also import own template, or search through

  4. Save the notebook to click “Create” button.

docs.title: Notebook Customization
docs.text:
(scrolling direction, toolbar customization, stylus palm rejection, sensitivity)

  1. Click on the ... to see more options

* annotate ... - you will see different options - current page info - rotate, change template (you can change template here again)

  1. Select Document Editing
  2. you can scroll down to Settings & select Document editing
  3. based on your preferences, toggle on/ off the one you like
  4. !!Tip!! - You are left handed -> you can change writing posture.
  5. !!Warning!! - if wrong posture setting -> can cause unexpected behaviour (eg. recognition error -> you are writing but it won't show)

Click ```((at the top right)) -> notebook setting -> click “Document editing” button. Customization -> Document editing settings (scrolling direction, toolbar customization, stylus palm rejection, sensitivity) Bookmarks, outlines
